title: Implement workflow history event streaming
purpose: Workflow history events published to per-workflow channels for live streaming and cursor-based replay. This decouples history consumers (monitoring dashboards, debugging tools, operators) from the event store API. The recorder publishes each event to the workflow's history channel after writing to the event store -- the event store remains the source of truth for recovery, the history channel is the read path for observation. Consumers subscribe to channels instead of querying the store directly, eliminating read contention under load and removing coupling to the storage layer's API.
task: Create history.rs in crates/liminal/src/aion/. This module handles per-workflow history channel lifecycle (create when workflow starts, populated as events occur), recorder integration (publish events to channel after event store write), cursor-based replay from the durable channel stream, and consumer subscription. Use the channel naming from AION-001 channels.rs (history_channel constructor). The history channel is a read-only projection of the event store -- it does NOT replace the event store as the source of truth for recovery. This brief does NOT implement history compaction, retention policies, or event store modifications.

requirements.title: Per-workflow history channel creation
requirements.spec: WHEN a workflow starts, THE SYSTEM SHALL create a durable history channel named by history_channel(namespace, workflow_id) from channels.rs. The channel SHALL persist events as a stream that supports cursor-based reads. WHEN the channel already exists (workflow restart/recovery), THE SYSTEM SHALL reuse the existing channel and its accumulated history.
acceptance: Starting a workflow creates a durable channel named aion.history.{namespace}.{workflow_id}
The channel is configured as durable (events persist across subscriber disconnections)
Restarting a workflow that already has a history channel reuses the existing channel with all prior events intact
The channel supports multiple concurrent subscribers

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:1 — target module is only a stub; all lifecycle/session/codec code for history belongs here unless it nears CN1's 500-line limit.
crates/liminal/src/aion/channels.rs:50-60 — use history_channel(namespace, workflow_id); do not hand-format aion.history.* names outside channels.rs.

crates/liminal/src/aion/signal.rs:30-93,170-204,327-364 — closest lifecycle pattern: Context owns Arc<Mutex<...>> registry, creates/reuses per-workflow ChannelHandle, and returns a public channel handle type.

crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:24-41,43-59,138-146 — ChannelConfig carries name/schema/mode, ChannelHandle::new creates an in-memory actor, and subscribe supports multiple subscribers but currently has no cursor argument.

crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:24-57,137-139 — recover_durable_channel reconstructs partition sequences from store using channel_id:partition stream keys; use this idea for restart/recovery reuse rather than overwriting prior durable stream state.
context: history_channel already enforces the exact aion.{kind}.{namespace}.{id} convention and validates empty/dotted namespace/workflow ids via AionSurfaceError::InvalidChannelName.

ChannelMode has Durable, but ChannelHandle::new currently backs both modes with the same in-memory ChannelActor; true persisted stream behavior must use durability primitives or a history-local projection store rather than assuming ChannelMode::Durable alone persists events.

SignalContext is the main convention to mirror: public context struct, Default with no-op dependencies, Debug implemented manually with finish_non_exhaustive, BTreeMap keyed by channel_name.as_str(), and module-private Session structs deriving Debug/Clone.
mod.rs is already compliant with CN3 and currently exports the module plus core Aion types only; if adding public history API re-exports, keep it to pub use lines only.
scout.approach: Implement HistoryContext in history.rs with an Arc<Mutex<HistoryRegistry>> mapping validated ChannelName strings to HistorySession objects. Add a start_workflow_history(context/config or namespace/workflow_id) API that calls history_channel, checks the registry for an existing session, and otherwise creates a ChannelHandle with ChannelConfig::new(channel_name, history schema, ChannelMode::Durable). For durability/restart semantics, do not treat an in-memory ChannelHandle as sufficient: either wrap/recover a single-partition DurableChannel using recover_durable_channel(channel_name, 1, store) supplied by the context, or hide a HistoryProjection trait behind the context that can append/read HistoryEvent bytes. Return a public HistoryChannel type exposing channel_name/handle/mode as Debug, and ensure creation/reuse tests assert the mode is Durable and repeated start returns the same accumulated history rather than resetting state.
scout.notes: The brief says create history.rs, but the file already exists as a stub. Also beware that channel::registry.rs and channel::supervisor.rs are stubs, so there is no central channel registry to rely on; history.rs must own its own registry or introduce only local abstractions. If history.rs grows near 500 lines, split with #[path = "history/types.rs"] submodules like signal.rs does, but that would add files beyond the brief and should be justified by CN1.

files_changed.note: Replaced the history stub with HistoryContext orchestration, workflow history channel start/reuse, and free functions.
Added HistoryChannel and supporting history session/registry types.
Added an in-memory durable projection registry that preserves per-channel history across subscribers/restarts within the context.
Added history channel schema and HistoryEvent encoding support.
Added creation/reuse tests for durable named history channels.
Re-exported public history APIs using pub use only.

dev.how: HistoryContext now resolves names via history_channel(namespace, workflow_id), creates ChannelHandle values with ChannelMode::Durable and the validated aion.history.{namespace}.{workflow_id} name, and maintains an Arc<Mutex<HistoryRegistry>> keyed by the validated channel name so repeated workflow starts reuse the same session. The private projection stores accumulated HistoryEvent values per channel and subscribe/start tests prove durable mode, naming, reuse, and retained prior events.
dev.deviation: The brief listed history.rs as the target file, but the implementation was split into private #[path = "history/*.rs"] submodules and a pub-use-only mod.rs export because the single-module implementation exceeded CN1's 500-line limit; this split was explicitly anticipated by the scout notes.

checklist.note: start_workflow_history creates/reuses a durable channel named through history_channel, yielding aion.history.{namespace}.{workflow_id}.

stories.note: The Aion engine can publish workflow history events to a durable per-workflow channel projection for consumers that do not call the event store.

acceptance.criterion: Starting a workflow creates a durable channel named aion.history.{namespace}.{workflow_id}
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:78-84 resolves the name via history_channel(namespace, workflow_id); crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:180-184 creates the ChannelConfig using channel_name.as_str() and ChannelMode::Durable; test aion::history::tests::start_creates_durable_named_channel_and_reuses_history asserts aion.history.prod.wf-1 at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:12-15.

acceptance.criterion: The channel is configured as durable (events persist across subscriber disconnections)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:180-184 configures ChannelMode::Durable; crates/liminal/src/aion/history/projection.rs:18-63 maintains a per-channel stream and replays appended events independent of live subscriber handles; test aion::history::tests::start_creates_durable_named_channel_and_reuses_history publishes before subscribing and receives the prior event at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:16-21.

acceptance.criterion: Restarting a workflow that already has a history channel reuses the existing channel with all prior events intact
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:171-188 looks up an existing session before creating one; crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:202-210 uses registry.active.entry(...).or_insert(session) to reuse the stored session; test aion::history::tests::start_creates_durable_named_channel_and_reuses_history restarts and receives sequence 1 at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:16-21.

acceptance.criterion: The channel supports multiple concurrent subscribers
acceptance.evidence: test aion::history::tests::multiple_subscribers_receive_live_history_independently creates two subscriptions and both receive the same live HistoryEvent at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:99-110; core fan-out keeps all active subscribers at crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:95-110.

fixes: Added explicit regression coverage for independent concurrent history subscribers in crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:99-110.

requirements.title: Recorder publishes events to history channel
requirements.spec: WHEN the Aion recorder writes an event to the event store, THE SYSTEM SHALL publish a HistoryEvent to the workflow's history channel after the event store write succeeds. The HistoryEvent SHALL include the sequence number, event type, timestamp, and payload. IF the history channel publish fails, THE SYSTEM SHALL log the failure but SHALL NOT fail the event store write -- the event store is the source of truth, the channel is a projection. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT publish to the history channel before the event store write succeeds.
acceptance: Each event written to the event store is followed by a HistoryEvent published to the history channel
The HistoryEvent sequence number matches the event store sequence number
The HistoryEvent event_type and payload match the event store record
A history channel publish failure does not cause the event store write to fail or roll back
A history channel publish failure is logged with enough context to diagnose (workflow_id, sequence, error)
No HistoryEvent is published before its corresponding event store write succeeds

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:1 — add the recorder integration seam here; keep event-store-specific details out of the public API.
crates/liminal/src/aion/types.rs:56-67 — HistoryEvent fields are sequence, event_type, timestamp, payload and must be the values published to subscribers.

crates/liminal/src/aion/dispatch.rs:147-155,259-300,375-380 — existing recorder-compatible pattern records operations after the action that caused them; history publish should happen after successful store append, never before.

crates/liminal/src/aion/signal.rs:206-232 — signal publishing pattern: encode typed payload, ChannelHandle::publish, then record durable operation only for ChannelMode::Durable; useful as a contrast because history must not fail the prior store write if projection publish fails.
crates/liminal/src/aion/error.rs:60-71,84-91 — use StreamingFailed for history publish/subscribe errors and ChannelLifecycleError for creation/reuse lifecycle failures.
context: HistoryEvent already implements Clone + Debug + PartialEq + Eq through aion/types.rs, satisfying public type convention; no new public HistoryEvent definition is needed.

AionSurfaceError::StreamingFailed contains channel_name, workflow_id, and message, which is appropriate for publish/subscribe diagnostics; but R2 requires publish failures to be logged/not returned, so do not propagate this error from the event-store write path.

The liminal crate does not currently depend on tracing/log; only liminal-server has tracing. If the implementation needs structured logging, it will require a Cargo.toml dependency change or a history-local injectable reporter/test sink; otherwise direct tracing::warn! will not compile in liminal.

There is no concrete Aion recorder/event-store type in this crate yet; existing dispatch/signal integrations use traits (DispatchRecorder, SignalRecorder) as seams rather than importing haematite public APIs directly.
scout.approach: Add a public recorder-facing method such as publish_recorded_event(&self, namespace, workflow_id, record: HistoryEvent) or a small HistoryRecorder/HistoryProjection trait that Aion calls only after its event-store append returns a sequence. The method should resolve/reuse the workflow history session, encode the HistoryEvent to bytes, append/publish to the durable history projection, and fan out to live subscribers; on publish/fanout error it should call an injectable logger/reporter with workflow_id, sequence, channel_name, and error, then return Ok(()) so the caller's store write is not rolled back. Tests should use a fake event-store append closure or ordered fake recorder to prove the append succeeds first, the sequence is copied into HistoryEvent.sequence, and a forced publish failure is reported but not returned.
scout.notes: R2 wording assumes an Aion recorder write already exists, but this repository only has recorder traits for dispatch/signal and a generic haematite-backed durability store. Avoid adding event-store modifications or exposing haematite::EventStore in history.rs public signatures; keep the integration additive and trait-based. Also ensure any sequence conversion preserves the store-assigned sequence; do not use the channel's own append sequence if it would be zero-based while workflow history sequences in tests are 1..N.

files_changed.note: Added publish_after_record/publish_recorded_event recorder-facing seams and non-fatal publish failure reporting.
Added HistoryPublishFailure and HistoryPublishReporter public diagnostic types.
Added projection append behavior and no-op reporter default.
Added HistoryEvent JSON encoding with sequence, event_type, timestamp, and payload.
Added tests proving append callback happens before projection publish and projection publish failure is reported but not returned.
Re-exported recorder-facing history publish helpers.

dev.how: publish_history_after_record accepts an append callback and only publishes the returned HistoryEvent after that callback succeeds, preserving the event-store-assigned sequence and payload fields. publish_recorded_event appends to the history projection and then fans out through the live channel; any encode/projection/fanout failure is converted into HistoryPublishFailure with channel_name, workflow_id, sequence, and error and sent to the injected reporter while returning Ok(()) so the source-of-truth event-store write is not rolled back.
dev.deviation: No event-store or haematite public API was added, per the scout notes and boundaries. Private submodules were added for CN1, and structured failure reporting is implemented via an injectable reporter instead of adding a new tracing/log dependency.

checklist.note: Recorder-facing APIs publish each already-written workflow event to the history channel only after the append callback succeeds; failed projection publish is reported and swallowed.

stories.note: The engine can project event-store records into workflow history channels after durable writes without coupling consumers to event-store APIs.

acceptance.criterion: Each event written to the event store is followed by a HistoryEvent published to the history channel
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:93-104 runs the durable append callback first and then calls publish_recorded_event with the returned HistoryEvent; crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:130-135 appends to the projection and fans out to the live channel; test aion::history::tests::publish_after_record_orders_append_before_projection verifies store then projection ordering at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:26-37.

acceptance.criterion: The HistoryEvent sequence number matches the event store sequence number
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:102-104 publishes the exact HistoryEvent returned by the append callback; crates/liminal/src/aion/history/codec.rs:47-51 encodes event.sequence directly; test aion::history::tests::publish_after_record_orders_append_before_projection returns event(7) and observes sequence 7 at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:29-37.

acceptance.criterion: The HistoryEvent event_type and payload match the event store record
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:117-130 passes the original HistoryEvent into encode_history_event and projection.append; crates/liminal/src/aion/history/codec.rs:47-55 encodes event_type and payload directly from the record; crates/liminal/src/aion/history/codec.rs:86-91 decodes back into HistoryEvent. The typed live-delivery test compares the full expected event at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:92-95.

acceptance.criterion: A history channel publish failure does not cause the event store write to fail or roll back
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:122-137 catches encode/projection/fanout errors, reports them, and returns Ok(()); test aion::history::tests::publish_failure_is_reported_without_returning_error asserts result.is_ok() after a forced projection failure at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:62-75.

acceptance.criterion: A history channel publish failure is logged with enough context to diagnose (workflow_id, sequence, error)
acceptance.evidence: Fixed in harden pass: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:224-245 now constructs HistoryPublishFailure with channel_name, workflow_id, sequence, and error, then emits tracing::warn! with those fields; crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:69-74 verifies diagnostic context; crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:20 adds tracing.

acceptance.criterion: No HistoryEvent is published before its corresponding event store write succeeds
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:102 uses append()? before line 103 publishes, so a failing append returns immediately; test aion::history::tests::publish_after_record_does_not_publish_when_append_fails asserts an append error and an empty subscription at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:41-58.

issues: Publish failures were only sent to the injectable HistoryPublishReporter; there was no actual log emission, so the 'is logged' criterion was not met until the harden pass.
HistoryPublishFailure was a public failure diagnostic but did not implement std::error::Error via thiserror, weakening CN4 compliance for public error/failure types.
There was no test proving a failed event-store append prevented history publication.
fixes: Added tracing = "0.1" to crates/liminal/Cargo.toml and Cargo.lock, and added structured tracing::warn! in crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:238-244 with channel_name, workflow_id, sequence, and error.

Added a regression test for append-failure/no-publish behavior at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:41-58 and strengthened failure-context assertions at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:69-74.

requirements.title: Cursor-based replay from durable channel
requirements.spec: WHEN a consumer subscribes to a workflow's history channel with a cursor position, THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver all events from that cursor position forward. WHEN a consumer subscribes with no cursor (or cursor 0), THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver the complete event history from the beginning. The cursor SHALL be the HistoryEvent sequence number. WHEN a consumer is caught up to the latest event, THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver new events in real time as they are published.
acceptance: Subscribing with cursor=5 to a channel with events 1..10 delivers events 6..10
Subscribing with no cursor delivers all events from sequence 1
Subscribing with cursor=0 delivers all events from sequence 1
After catching up to the latest event, new events published by the recorder are delivered in real time
Multiple consumers with different cursor positions each receive the correct event subset

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:1 — implement subscribe/replay handle and cursor filtering in this module.
crates/liminal/src/channel/subscription.rs:10-40 — current SubscriptionHandle only queues live Envelope values and try_next returns Option<Envelope>; it has no replay or typed HistoryEvent support.

crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:34-45,47-74,95-110 — publish only fans out to currently registered subscribers and drops disconnected weak refs; existing actor does not retain history for later cursor reads.

crates/liminal/src/durability/replay.rs:3-38 — replay_from reads durable MessageEnvelope batches from an offset until head; useful if history stores encoded HistoryEvents as durable channel envelopes.
crates/liminal/src/durability/store.rs:16-33 — DurableStore::read_from(stream_key, offset, limit) is the low-level cursor read primitive available behind an internal context.
context: The cursor required by the brief is HistoryEvent.sequence, not necessarily DurableStore entry.sequence or durable channel partition offset. If the store/channel is zero-based, map cursor=5 to events with event.sequence > 5, not read_from offset 5 blindly unless you guarantee alignment.

The existing live channel subscription has no blocking/async stream implementation; all current tests use try_next. A history subscription handle should likely mirror this with try_next() -> Result<Option<HistoryEvent>, AionSurfaceError> while internally draining a replay queue first and then live subscription.

Multiple concurrent subscribers are naturally supported by ChannelActor's Vec of weak subscriber registrations, but each subscriber needs independent replay state; do not put a shared cursor in the session.
Durable replay helpers deserialize MessageEnvelope, not HistoryEvent. You need a history codec layer that decodes the envelope.payload into HistoryEvent, then filters by HistoryEvent.sequence.
scout.approach: Create a HistorySubscription handle type that owns a VecDeque<HistoryEvent> replay backlog plus a live SubscriptionHandle and channel/workflow context for error mapping. subscribe_history should resolve the channel, compute start_after = cursor.unwrap_or(0), load durable events from the session/projection, filter sequence > start_after, register a live subscription, and return the handle. To avoid a race between replay and live subscription, register the live SubscriptionHandle before or atomically with reading the durable backlog, then de-duplicate by HistoryEvent.sequence in try_next so events published during catch-up are not missed or double-delivered. try_next should yield replay events first, then decode live envelopes and continue yielding real-time events.
scout.notes: The acceptance examples use events 1..10 and cursor=5 => 6..10; this is exclusive-after cursor semantics. If the implementation uses DurableStore::read_from offsets, no cursor means 0 but HistoryEvent sequence 0 should still probably be treated as start-from-beginning per brief. Concurrency gotcha: a simple read-then-subscribe implementation can miss an event appended between the read and subscribe; tests should cover publishing while a subscriber is catching up if feasible.

files_changed.note: Added subscribe_history cursor handling and projection replay wiring.
Added HistorySubscription with replay backlog, live SubscriptionHandle, sequence de-duplication, and typed try_next.
Added read_after(channel_name, cursor) projection API using HistoryEvent.sequence exclusive-after semantics.
Added live envelope decoding back into HistoryEvent values.
Added tests for cursor=5 delivering 6..10, no cursor and cursor=0 delivering full history, different subscribers, and live delivery after catch-up.

dev.how: subscribe_history registers a live channel subscription, reads the durable projection with exclusive-after HistoryEvent.sequence semantics, and returns a HistorySubscription. HistorySubscription drains replay first, then decodes live envelopes into HistoryEvent values while tracking last_sequence so events published during catch-up are not double-delivered.
dev.deviation: The implementation uses a history-local projection store rather than the lower-level durability replay helpers because the brief required HistoryEvent.sequence cursors and the existing channel SubscriptionHandle has no cursor API. This follows the scouted alternative of hiding a history projection behind the context.

checklist.note: History subscribers can read all events with no cursor or cursor 0, events after an arbitrary HistoryEvent.sequence cursor, and then receive live events.

stories.note: Operators can stream workflow history from typed channel subscriptions for dashboards/debug tooling without querying the event store.

acceptance.criterion: Subscribing with cursor=5 to a channel with events 1..10 delivers events 6..10
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:157-168 passes cursor.unwrap_or(0) into the subscription; crates/liminal/src/aion/history/projection.rs:47-63 reads events with event.sequence > cursor; test aion::history::tests::cursor_replay_and_live_delivery_are_typed_and_independent asserts cursor 5 yields 6..10 at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:80-88.

acceptance.criterion: Subscribing with no cursor delivers all events from sequence 1
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:157 sets None to cursor 0; test aion::history::tests::cursor_replay_and_live_delivery_are_typed_and_independent asserts None yields 1..10 at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:84-90.

acceptance.criterion: Subscribing with cursor=0 delivers all events from sequence 1
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history/projection.rs:54-59 filters event.sequence > cursor; test aion::history::tests::cursor_replay_and_live_delivery_are_typed_and_independent asserts Some(0) yields 1..10 at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:86-90.

acceptance.criterion: After catching up to the latest event, new events published by the recorder are delivered in real time
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history/types.rs:89-109 drains replay first, then reads live SubscriptionHandle envelopes and decodes HistoryEvent values; test aion::history::tests::cursor_replay_and_live_delivery_are_typed_and_independent subscribes at cursor 10, publishes event 11, and receives the full expected event at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:92-95.

acceptance.criterion: Multiple consumers with different cursor positions each receive the correct event subset
acceptance.evidence: test aion::history::tests::cursor_replay_and_live_delivery_are_typed_and_independent creates consumers at cursor 5, None, and 0 and asserts 6..10, 1..10, and 1..10 respectively at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:84-90.

fixes: Strengthened the live-delivery regression test to compare the full HistoryEvent, not only sequence, at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:92-95.

requirements.title: Consumer decoupling from event store
requirements.spec: History consumers SHALL read workflow events exclusively through channel subscription. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose the event store API to history consumers. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a subscribe function that takes a workflow's namespace and workflow_id, resolves the history channel name, and returns a subscription handle with cursor support. The subscription handle SHALL deliver HistoryEvent values, not raw event store records.
acceptance: The subscribe_history(namespace, workflow_id, cursor) function returns a subscription handle
The subscription handle yields HistoryEvent values (the types from AION-001 types.rs)
No event store types (EventStore, StoreError) appear in the history module's public API
A consumer can stream a workflow's complete history using only the subscription handle -- no event store import required

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:1 — public subscribe_history API and public subscription handle must live here with no EventStore/StoreError public exposure.
crates/liminal/src/aion/mod.rs:1-14 — add only pub use re-exports for HistoryContext/HistoryChannel/HistorySubscription if desired; keep mod.rs logic-free.
crates/liminal/src/aion/types.rs:56-67 — subscription handle should yield these HistoryEvent values, not haematite events or liminal Envelope values.
crates/liminal/src/aion/signal.rs:273-325 — established convention for free functions delegating to explicit context methods, useful for subscribe_history(context, namespace, workflow_id, cursor).

crates/liminal/src/durability/error.rs:1-18 and crates/liminal/src/durability/store.rs:45-57 — these internal types wrap haematite/EventStore details; map them to AionSurfaceError internally rather than exposing them.
context: Aion modules expose typed domain APIs and hide lower-level liminal primitives where possible; signal.rs exposes SignalPayload and SignalChannel, while its recorder trait returns AionSurfaceError rather than haematite errors.

Acceptance explicitly forbids EventStore and StoreError in history module public API. Internal use of DurableStore/DurabilityError is safer because those are liminal abstractions, but even those should not be required for consumers calling subscribe_history.

SubscriptionHandle from channel module yields raw Envelope; exposing it directly would fail R4 because consumers would need to decode raw channel bytes. Wrap it in a HistorySubscription that yields HistoryEvent.
The channel naming resolver must remain inside subscribe_history by calling history_channel(namespace, workflow_id); consumers should not pass raw channel names.
scout.approach: Define pub fn subscribe_history(context: &HistoryContext, namespace: &str, workflow_id: &str, cursor: Option<u64>) -> Result<HistorySubscription, AionSurfaceError> and a matching HistoryContext::subscribe_history method. HistorySubscription should expose a typed try_next (or iterator-like method consistent with existing non-async handles) returning Result<Option<HistoryEvent>, AionSurfaceError>. Keep any store/projection traits private or constructor-only on HistoryContext; the consumer path should only need liminal::aion::{subscribe_history, HistoryEvent}. Map all internal LiminalError/DurabilityError/serde_json errors through StreamingFailed with channel_name and workflow_id context.
scout.notes: The verification grep in the brief is imprecise because comments/internal private fields may mention EventStore; the key is no event-store types in public signatures. Since HistoryEvent contains SystemTime, JSON encoding needs a deterministic representation (for example epoch millis/u64) and careful checked conversions on decode; serde_json cannot directly serialize SystemTime without custom conversion.

files_changed.note: Added public subscribe_history(context, namespace, workflow_id, cursor) and HistoryContext::subscribe_history APIs.
Added public HistorySubscription yielding typed HistoryEvent values from try_next.
Kept raw envelope decoding internal so consumers receive HistoryEvent values only.
Added consumer-facing subscription tests that use only the history subscription handle.
Re-exported HistoryContext, HistoryChannel, HistorySubscription, and history free functions without adding logic to mod.rs.

dev.how: Consumers call subscribe_history with namespace/workflow_id/cursor; the function resolves the validated history channel internally and returns HistorySubscription. The public subscription API exposes typed HistoryEvent values through try_next and no EventStore, StoreError, haematite, DurableStore, or raw Envelope types appear in the history module public consumer signatures.
dev.deviation: mod.rs was modified to add pub use re-exports for ergonomics while preserving CN3. The implementation deliberately avoided public event-store types and kept projection/storage internals private.

checklist.note: History consumers are decoupled from the event store and read typed HistoryEvent values exclusively through subscribe_history/HistorySubscription.

stories.note: A consumer can stream complete or cursored workflow history through the history subscription handle with no event-store import.

acceptance.criterion: The subscribe_history(namespace, workflow_id, cursor) function returns a subscription handle
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:145-168 implements HistoryContext::subscribe_history returning HistorySubscription; crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:312-319 exposes the free subscribe_history(context, namespace, workflow_id, cursor) function.

acceptance.criterion: The subscription handle yields HistoryEvent values (the types from AION-001 types.rs)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/history/types.rs:89 declares try_next as Result<Option<HistoryEvent>, AionSurfaceError>; crates/liminal/src/aion/history/types.rs:101-107 decodes live envelopes and returns typed HistoryEvent values.

acceptance.criterion: No event store types (EventStore, StoreError) appear in the history module's public API
acceptance.evidence: grep -rn 'EventStore\|StoreError' crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs crates/liminal/src/aion/history returned no matches; public history functions use HistoryContext, HistoryEvent, HistorySubscription, and AionSurfaceError at crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs:268-319.

acceptance.criterion: A consumer can stream a workflow's complete history using only the subscription handle -- no event store import required
acceptance.evidence: test aion::history::tests::cursor_replay_and_live_delivery_are_typed_and_independent streams complete history via subscribe_history and drain at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:84-90 and crates/liminal/src/aion/history/tests.rs:113-118. Hardened public HistoryChannel now exposes only channel_name and mode, not a raw ChannelHandle, at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/types.rs:13-17.

issues: HistoryChannel originally exposed a public raw ChannelHandle, which allowed consumers to subscribe to raw envelopes and bypass the typed HistorySubscription path. That conflicted with the requirement that history consumers read exclusively through channel subscription handles yielding HistoryEvent values.
fixes: Removed the public raw ChannelHandle from HistoryChannel and adjusted HistorySession::to_channel; final public HistoryChannel fields are only channel_name and mode at crates/liminal/src/aion/history/types.rs:13-17.

boundaries: SHALL NOT replace the event store as the source of truth for workflow recovery -- the history channel is a read projection.
SHALL NOT implement history compaction or retention policies.
SHALL NOT implement event store modifications -- the recorder integration is additive (publish after write).
SHALL NOT implement activity dispatch -- that is AION-002.
SHALL NOT implement signal delivery -- that is AION-003.
SHALL NOT expose event store types in the history module's public API.
verification: cargo test for the history module passes (unit tests for channel creation, recorder publish, cursor replay, consumer subscription).
No file in crates/liminal/src/aion/ exceeds 500 lines.
grep -rn 'EventStore\|StoreError' crates/liminal/src/aion/history.rs returns no matches in the public API (internal wiring may reference recorder).
A history channel publish failure does not fail the event store write (verified by test that forces channel error and asserts event store write succeeds).
Cursor-based replay delivers the correct event subset for arbitrary cursor positions (verified by parameterized test).
